exercises.description: Order the sentences (B-G) below to make a story. {v} The first sentence of the story (A) is given for you as an example. {v} A.-Robert De Niro was born on August 17, 1943 in Manhattan, New York City.

questions.title: B He was trained at the Stella Adler Conservatory and the American Workshop.
C De Niro´s last prizes are the AFI Lifetime Achievement Award in 2003 and the Golden Globe Cecil B. DeMille Award in 2010.
D He received an Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor for his role in The Godfather Part II in 1974.
E De Niro first gained fame for his role in Bang the Drum Slowly (1973).
F His paternal grandfather was Italian, and his other ancestry is English, Dutch, German, French and Irish.
G He also received the Academy Award for Best Actor for his role as Jake LaMotta in Raging Bull.

exercises.heading: Fashion
exercises.description: Person A {v} Back in the day people didn’t really use fashion as a way to express themselves. Everyone had their role and they stuck to it - there wasn’t much choice on what clothes you wore. Times have obviously changed and I’m not sure it’s been for the better. Today, people seem to be extremely occupied by trying to express themselves through their clothes and accessories and don’t pay enough attention to things like their career. {v} Person B {v} Since I’ve been working in the fashion industry for many years, I see different types of fashion every day. It’s great when the designers do a good job expressing their ideas and thoughts through their work, but sometimes they don’t do a good job. When they have done a good job, however, it is incredible. But, if we’re honest, the fashion industry can sometimes be amazing, and other times be terrible. You don’t know what you will find. {v} Person C {v} I love to express myself through my clothes. I put time in daily to choose the right blouse, skirt, pants or dress to wear for the day. My wardrobe is immense and I couldn’t be happier about it. If you asked me about the most important factor of fashion though, I would tell you that the outfit itself isn’t where most of the personal expression comes from. Accessories and make-up can make a bigger statement than any other parts. {v} Person D {v} I do my best to sympathize with those who love fashion, and I do understand the benefits of having a variety of different clothing items. Call me old-fashioned or practical, but I think it's a lot of washing for the people who walk out the front door with a new look every day. I do appreciate accessories, especially on a sunny day, but it makes me think about how our focus has become so materialistic.
exercises.subtitle: Four people comment on an article written about how important fashion is nowadays. Read their comments and answer the questions below.
exercises.label: Reading Part 4

questions.title: 1. Who thinks there is too much focus on fashion and not enough on working a job?
2. Who thinks that fashion can be an expression of art?
3. Who spends time on the way they look every day?
4. Who thinks that clothes aren’t as important as accessories?
5. Who considers fashion to be impractical?
6. Who thinks that fashion focuses too much on material things?
7. Who has a lot of experience in fashion?

exercises.description: Read the text below. Match the headings A-H to the paragraphs 19-25.
exercises.label: Reading Part 5

answers: F.Popular fields of study in the U.S .
questions.title: 19. The grading system in the UK is quite different to that of the United States. The GCSE grading {v} system is used by schools in England, Wales, and Northern Ireland. The grading scale for {v} GCSE exams ranges from 1-9, with 9 been the highest and 1 the lowest. Students who achieve {v} 9 will get a grade of A*. Students who score 4 and above will achieve a ‘standard pass.’ {v} Grade 5 and above is considered a ‘strong pass.’ English Literature, English Language, and {v} Mathematics will first be graded this way from 2017. Students who do not score at least 4 in {v} English and Mathematics must re-sit these examinations.

answers: C. How the U.S. system differs
questions.title: 20. In contrast, the American school system begins with children entering kindergarten around age {v} 5, and graduating the 12th grade around age 18. Grading is on a scale where teachers {v} assign a letter grade. A, B, and C are passing. On rare occasions, a C minus is counted as {v} passing, but often, scoring below a C, a C minus, D, or F, would mean failing the course and {v} require the student to retake it. In the four years of high school, these letter grades are assigned {v} a numerical value and contribute to the overall GPA, grade point average, of the student. The {v} highest possible GPA is 4.0.

21. There are many different courses that one can study at university level in one of the 100 plus {v} universities throughout the UK. Universities in the UK are also known as Recognised Bodies. {v} Some of the subjects available at university include law, psychology, paramedical science, {v} computer science, sports psychology, medicine, and marine biology. The Universities and {v} Colleges Admissions Service (UCAS) is the body which manages UK university undergraduate {v} applications. The total mean cost of studying in the UK amounts to about 22 thousand {v} Pounds a year. On average, an undergraduate course lasts for 3 years. Master's degree {v} courses last for 1 year. Whereas, in the U.S., the average undergraduate degree lasts 4 years, {v} and a Master’s 2 years.

answers: D. Students who attend and those who do not attend
questions.title: 22. There are two different study options that students can choose: on-site learning and distance {v} learning. A student who chooses to attend must be present in classes on campus and can be {v} penalised if they fail to do so, as attendance levels are monitored. Valid reasons must be given {v} if absence is unavoidable. Attending lectures and seminars in person is shown to help {v} improve learning abilities. Distance students just have to use online study material, and appear {v} in person for examinations. Many leading colleges offer distance learning courses to {v} students. Though not the preferred method of learning for many, it is a useful alternative.

answers: G. What is university meant to prepare you for?
questions.title: 23. Education in the UK follows some distinct methods. In the case of degree prog rammes, some {v} core modules are compulsory. These must be attended by the students. However, students {v} have the option of choosing secondary or optional modules. Tutorials and seminars are held, {v} as well as lectures. Assignments and presentations are often used to grade students, as well {v} as examinations. But the basic idea remains, regardless of course format, that the UK {v} educational system aims to make students ready for jobs or further education. Graduating {v} earlier and with less student debt, as compared to the U.S., sets the graduate up for greater {v} success in the workplace.

24. Some of the most popular fields of study in the UK are humanities, economics, political {v} relations, medicine and international studies. Many career-based courses have proven to be {v} useful for moving into a job straight after university. Newer fields of studies frequently create {v} even more opportunities for students. UK fields of study are engineered to help students get {v} ready for specific professional ambitions. Most students in the UK apply for courses in the {v} domains of business, health, biological sciences, social studies, followed closely by education {v} and arts.

25. 18-year-old students are the most likely candidates to apply to universities. About 15 percent of {v} undergraduate and 40 percent of graduate students are from outside the UK. China, India, {v} Nigeria, and many EU countries send students for UK university courses. Over 80 percent of {v} students reported that they were satisfied with their higher education. About 90 percent of {v} students start working, or go on to further studies after their course. The number of students {v} keeps increasing every year. Study abroad programmes are also wildly popular throughout {v} Europe, bringing students in to the UK from all over Europe for up to one year of exchange {v} study.

exercises.description: This is the Aptis Listening Test.
exercises.label: Listening Part 2
exercises.title: 15. Listen to a man and a woman discuss their opinions of ageing. Read the opinions below and decide whose opinion matches the statements made by the man, the woman or both the man and the woman. {v} Who expresses which opinion? {x}

questions.title: Question 1: What is his opinion on the impact a prison has on someone's life? {x}
answers: They allow prisoners to learn new skills that they may be able to use outside the prison.
They serve to give prisoners an opportunity to see what they have done wrong.
They help prisoners to understand the value of work.

questions.title: Question 2: What is his opinion on a family member's role for prisoners? {x}
answers: It is unhealthy for a family to continue to be in contact with a family member in prison.
It is important for a prisoner's family to support them financially while they are in prison
It is sad when prisoners lose contact with their families while they are in prison.

exercises.description: This is the Aptis Listening Test.
exercises.label: Listening Part 3
exercises.title: 16. Listen to a man explain the purpose of prisons or correctional facilities and answer the questions below. {x}

questions.title: Question 1: What does the expert think about drones? {x}
answers: She thinks they are quite dangerous and should only be used in certain, authorized areas.
She thinks that they can be used as a way to keep the lives of humans safe while performing dangerous jobs.
She thinks they are taking jobs away from people and will cause more problems than help in the future.

questions.title: Question 2: What does the expert think about the future of drones? {x}
answers: She thinks that they will be extremely helpful as they develop further because they can perform tasks that humans cannot.
She thinks that it is too easy to violate someone's privacy while using a drone and they should be discontinued until further improvement.
She thinks that as drones develop further, they should only be available to the government for specific use.

title: Word Explorer
description: Get around, make decisions, and share what you know — every word you use here is a step closer to fluency.
